title: Cross-file step numbering mismatch between reviews.md and big-head-skeleton.md
description: reviews.md uses Step 0/0a/1/2/3/4 for Big Head consolidation protocol while big-head-skeleton.md uses steps 1-9. No documented mapping between the two schemes. Cross-references like 'see steps 8-9' in RULES.md:103 and pantry.md:252 require consulting two files. Fix: add one-line mapping note (e.g. 'reviews.md Step 4 = skeleton steps 8-9: SendMessage to Pest Control, await verdict'). Acceptance: reader can look up any cross-file step reference and find corresponding content.

title: fix: GLOSSARY pre-push hook entry omits sync-to-claude.sh details
description:
GLOSSARY.md:58 defines the pre-push hook as syncing "agents/*.md to ~/.claude/agents/ and orchestration/ files to ~/.claude/orchestration/" — but omits:
- The _archive/ exclusion from rsync
- That only 2 of 6 scripts (fill-review-slots.sh, compose-review-skeletons.sh) are synced
- The CLAUDE.md copy step
- The non-delete policy (preserving custom files in ~/.claude/orchestration/)

The script itself (sync-to-claude.sh:23-44) documents all of this inline. The GLOSSARY entry is a summary, not a specification, so the gaps are cosmetic — sync behavior is not a runtime decision input.

Fix: Expand the GLOSSARY pre-push hook entry to note the key behaviors.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] GLOSSARY pre-push hook entry mentions _archive/ exclusion, selective script sync, CLAUDE.md copy, and non-delete policy

Audit reference: DRIFT-023 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: WWD mode selection rule missing from checkpoints.md
description: Root cause: checkpoints.md describes serial/batch WWD modes but omits the mode selection rule (present only in RULES.md:129-130). Pest Control reads checkpoints.md at runtime and has no instruction for choosing which mode applies. Also a dual-maintenance surface: two independently written descriptions of the same feature. Fix: Add mode selection rule to checkpoints.md WWD section, add cross-reference to RULES.md Step 3 as authoritative source. Found by: Correctness (CO-F001), Excellence (EX-F002).

title: README.md architecture diagram does not capture Pest Control dual role
nitpicker.md frontmatter description too long; second sentence ignored by Scout
description:
Root cause: agents/nitpicker.md:3 YAML description grew to 2 sentences after adding scope fence details. Scout agent discovery (Step 2.5) extracts only the first sentence. The second sentence adds no functional value in frontmatter and makes nitpicker inconsistent with other single-sentence agent descriptions.

Affected surfaces:
- agents/nitpicker.md:3

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F2), Excellence (P3 - F8)

Fix: Shorten frontmatter description to one sentence. Move details to the body.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Frontmatter description is a single sentence
- [ ] No information loss (details moved to body)

title: fill-review-slots.sh: fill_all_slots block comment describes wrong map format
description: Block comment says NUL-terminated sentinel but actual format is tab-delimited newlines.

title: Skeleton format spec hints do not match Pantry actual output sections
description: The skeleton templates have format spec hints that do not exactly match the sections the Pantry actually writes. dirt-pusher-skeleton.md (line 30) says 'Sections: Context, Scope Boundaries, Focus' but the Pantry also writes a 'Summary Doc Sections' section. nitpicker-skeleton.md (line 19) says 'Sections: Scope, Files, Focus, Detailed Instructions' but the Pantry writes sections including commit range, file list, report output path, bead-filing prohibition, and messaging guidelines. Additionally, big-head-skeleton.md has no format hint at all, unlike the other two skeletons. Affected surfaces: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md:30, nitpicker-skeleton.md:19, big-head-skeleton.md:53. Fix: Update skeleton hints to match Pantry output, or add '(approximate)' qualifier. Add format hint to big-head-skeleton.md.

title: Placeholder Conventions Cleanup
description: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md issues + cross-template placeholder syntax inconsistencies. 19 P3 bugs covering enforcement gaps, naming ambiguity, audit staleness, and angle-bracket vs curly-brace convention drift.

title: Big Head failure artifact timestamp coexistence under re-spawn not documented
description: The Big Head failure artifact filename is review-consolidated-{TIMESTAMP}-FAILED.md, where {TIMESTAMP} is Queen-provided. If Big Head is re-spawned after failure, a new timestamp would be provided, so the old failure artifact and new success artifact coexist (correct by design). However, this dependency on fresh timestamps at re-spawn is not documented. Affected surfaces: big-head-skeleton.md:58, big-head-skeleton.md:20. Fix: No code change needed. Add a comment noting that both -FAILED and success files may coexist after a retry cycle, and that re-spawn requires a fresh TIMESTAMP value.

title: Dual placeholder conventions for round-conditional blocks
description: Root cause: pantry.md uses {PANTRY_ROUND_1_CHECK_START/END} braces while reviews.md uses <IF ROUND 1> angle brackets for the same conditional construct. Risk of unresolved markers in composed briefs. Affected files: orchestration/templates/pantry.md:382-416,399-419 and orchestration/templates/reviews.md:466-545. Fix: Standardize on one marker convention. Add Pantry self-check for leftover markers. Acceptance criteria: (1) One consistent marker convention. (2) Pantry validates composed briefs for leftover markers. Found by: Excellence (EX-1, EX-5 both P3).

title: fix: SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md uses stale Boss-Bot term and Claude Sonnet 4.5 model
description:
orchestration/templates/SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md uses outdated terminology:
- Line 8: "Boss-Bot: Claude Sonnet 4.5"
- Line 340: "Implementation files read in boss-bot window"
- Line 342: "Boss-bot stayed focused"

"Boss-Bot" has no GLOSSARY entry and is not used anywhere else in the framework. The orchestrator role is "Queen" per GLOSSARY.md:79 and RULES.md throughout. "Claude Sonnet 4.5" is both a stale model name and the wrong tier (Queen runs on opus).

Fix: Replace all Boss-Bot/boss-bot with Queen/the Queen. Replace "Claude Sonnet 4.5" with `opus` or point to the Model Assignments table. Consider archiving SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md if it is no longer actively used (the Scout now generates briefing.md automatically).

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] No "Boss-Bot" or "boss-bot" references in active templates
- [ ] Model reference updated to current tier

Audit reference: DRIFT-022 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Big Head polling loop: single-invocation constraint under-documented and timeout may be too short
AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md contain identical content with no sync mechanism documented
nitpicker.md NOT YOUR RESPONSIBILITY blocks use inconsistent scope vocabulary
description:
Root cause: nitpicker.md:52-56 the four 'NOT YOUR RESPONSIBILITY' sections describe other reviewers' scopes using different granularity and phrasing. For example, the Clarity block describes Excellence scope as 'performance inefficiencies, security vulnerabilities, architecture concerns, scalability issues, best practice violations' while the Correctness block says 'performance of correct code, architectural elegance, security hardening beyond functional correctness.' Same domain, different terms.

Affected surfaces:
- agents/nitpicker.md:52-56 (all 4 NOT YOUR RESPONSIBILITY sections)

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F1). Single reviewer, standalone finding.

Fix: Unify the ownership language so the same domain always uses the same shorthand labels across all 4 blocks.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Each reviewer's scope is described identically across all blocks that reference it

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md shell example uses unquoted SESSION_DIR variable
fill-review-slots.sh: undocumented assumptions in fill_all_slots (tabs in paths, printf %b)
description: fill_all_slots has several undocumented assumptions about input data format.

title: fix: MEMORY.md references deleted _session-3be37d without noting its absence is expected
description:
MEMORY.md:51 references "_session-3be37d" as the session where CLAUDE.md was synced after accidentally deleting a session directory. The session directory itself does not exist on disk (it was the one that was deleted).

The reference is correct as a historical narrative but could confuse someone grepping for session IDs and finding no matching directory.

Fix: Annotate the MEMORY.md reference: "(this session directory was accidentally deleted — absence is expected)."

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] MEMORY.md _session-3be37d reference annotated with expected-absence note

Audit reference: DRIFT-025 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS enforcement incomplete and angle-bracket syntax undocumented
Documentation & Setup
description: 11 P3 bugs across README.md, SETUP.md, AGENTS.md, installation guides. Covers pantry-review deprecation cleanup, missing file references, fork instructions, and formatting issues.

fix: scrub-pii.sh and pre-commit hook not described in SETUP.md or README.md
description:
The pre-commit hook runs scripts/scrub-pii.sh to strip email addresses from .beads/issues.jsonl before staging. This is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md:176-178 but absent from SETUP.md and README.md.

install-hooks.sh installs both hooks (pre-push for sync, pre-commit for PII scrub). Developers following SETUP.md will learn about the pre-push hook but not the pre-commit hook. They may not understand warnings from the pre-commit hook or why PII scrubbing exists.

Fix: Add a note to SETUP.md Quick Setup section explaining both hooks are installed.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] SETUP.md mentions both pre-push (sync) and pre-commit (PII scrub) hooks
- [ ] README.md optionally mentions PII scrubbing in the setup section

Dual-maintenance surface: Pest Control tool list in two files
description: Root cause: Pest Control tool list (Bash, Read, Write, Glob, Grep) is authoritative in checkpoints.md:L17 and README.md. Currently consistent but creates future drift risk. Fix: Designate one location as canonical, add cross-reference in the other. Found by: Excellence (EX-F005).

title: Pantry 'report to Queen immediately' misleading for Task subagent model
PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md audit claims All Files Pass despite partial nitpicker-skeleton.md
fix: GLOSSARY says 4 checkpoints but framework has 5 (SSV omitted)
description:
GLOSSARY.md:46 defines checkpoint as having "four checkpoints: CCO, WWD, DMVDC, and CCB." GLOSSARY.md:64 repeats "All four checkpoints are executed by Pest Control." checkpoints.md:15 lists five: "All checkpoint verifications (SSV, CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB) are executed by Pest Control." The Hard Gates table in RULES.md:255-263 has 7 rows (SSV, CCO impl, CCO review, WWD, DMVDC, CCB, Reviews).

Root cause: GLOSSARY.md was written before SSV was added as a checkpoint. The GLOSSARY was not updated when SSV was introduced. The GLOSSARY also does not acknowledge the CCO impl vs CCO review distinction.

Current behavior: The GLOSSARY is the stated single source of truth for term definitions. A reader using it as authoritative will be unaware of SSV and will not understand the two CCO configurations. Someone troubleshooting a failed SSV gate using the GLOSSARY will find no definition for it.

Fix: Update GLOSSARY.md checkpoint definition and acronym table to reflect all 5 checkpoints, note the CCO dual-configuration, and match the Hard Gates table.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:46: Change "four checkpoints" to "five checkpoints" and add SSV
- orchestration/GLOSSARY.md:64: Change "All four" to "All five"
- orchestration/GLOSSARY.md Checkpoint Acronyms table: Add SSV row

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] GLOSSARY lists all 5 checkpoints: SSV, CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB
- [ ] GLOSSARY notes CCO runs in two configurations (impl and review)
- [ ] Checkpoint Acronyms table includes SSV with expansion and description
- [ ] Count references ("four" → "five") updated throughout GLOSSARY

Audit reference: DRIFT-008 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: install-hooks.sh: pre-commit backup cp lacks error handling (asymmetric with pre-push)
description: The pre-commit hook backup at install-hooks.sh uses bare cp without error handling, while the pre-push backup was hardened. Cosmetic inconsistency.

title: RULES.md: mixed [[ and [ bracket styles in validation block
description: Validation code block uses both bash-specific [[ and POSIX [ brackets inconsistently.

title: Audit Findings: Orchestration Documentation Drift
description:
Findings from the systematic documentation drift audit performed on 2026-02-21. The audit cross-referenced all orchestration documentation (RULES.md, templates, CLAUDE.md, GLOSSARY, README, CONTRIBUTING) against the actual implementation (agent files, scripts, hooks, session artifacts) across 8 domains using 4 parallel audit agents.

Full audit report: docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

32 total findings: 4 S1 (Breaking), 15 S2 (Misleading), 13 S3 (Cosmetic).
This epic tracks the S1 and S2 fixes. S3 findings are documented in the audit report for opportunistic cleanup.

title: big-head.md agent definition lacks cross-reference to reviews.md protocol
fix: minor bd prohibition wording drift between CLAUDE.md and RULES.md
description:
CLAUDE.md:38 and RULES.md:16 both prohibit the same bd commands but with slightly different formatting:
- CLAUDE.md: "NEVER run `bd show`..." (plain text NEVER)
- RULES.md: "**NEVER** run `bd show`..." (bold NEVER)
- CLAUDE.md: "The Scout subagent does this."
- RULES.md: "the Scout does this"

Functionally identical scope — same 4 commands plus "any bd query command" catch-all. The drift is purely cosmetic.

Fix: Normalize wording across both files. Since sync-to-claude.sh copies project CLAUDE.md to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, only one source needs updating.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] bd prohibition text identical between CLAUDE.md and RULES.md

Audit reference: DRIFT-026 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: SETUP.md references wrong path for SESSION_PLAN_TEMPLATE.md
fix: remove 3 dead artifact entries from RULES.md Session Directory list
description:
RULES.md:345-347 lists three session artifacts that have never been created in any of the 13 observed sessions:

1. Line 345: `orchestrator-state*.md — orchestrator snapshots` — zero matches across all _session-* directories
2. Line 346: `step3b-transition-gate.md — review transition gate` — zero matches across all _session-* directories
3. Line 347: `HANDOFF-*.md — handoff documents` — zero matches. One session (_session-8ae30b) has an `EXECUTION_HANDOFF.md` at top level which does not match the documented glob pattern, suggesting an older workflow convention.

Root cause: These entries describe an earlier design that was either never implemented or was replaced by different mechanisms:
- orchestrator-state*.md was likely superseded by the queen-state.md template (orchestration/templates/queen-state.md exists)
- step3b-transition-gate.md describes a gate check that happens in RULES.md Step 3b logic but produces no artifact
- HANDOFF-*.md was likely superseded by session-summary.md and the "Hand off" step in CLAUDE.md

Additionally, two artifacts that ARE produced in every session are missing from the list:
- briefing.md — written by Scout (referenced at RULES.md:86, 99 but absent from artifact list)
- session-summary.md — written by Pantry (referenced in pantry.md:184 but absent from artifact list)

Fix: Remove the 3 dead entries and add the 2 missing ones.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md:345: Remove orchestrator-state*.md entry (or replace with queen-state.md if that is the intended artifact)
- orchestration/RULES.md:346: Remove step3b-transition-gate.md entry
- orchestration/RULES.md:347: Remove HANDOFF-*.md entry
- orchestration/RULES.md:343-349: Add briefing.md and session-summary.md entries

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] No dead artifact entries remain in RULES.md Session Directory list
- [ ] briefing.md listed with note "written by Scout (Step 1a)"
- [ ] session-summary.md listed with note "written by Pantry (optional)"
- [ ] Every artifact listed in RULES.md can be found in at least one actual session directory

Audit reference: DRIFT-012, DRIFT-013, DRIFT-014, DRIFT-024 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Retry count asymmetry between RULES.md (1 retry) and reviews.md (2-attempt) undocumented
parse-progress-log.sh: dead branch comment could be more precise
description: The UNREACHABLE comment could better explain why the branch is unreachable.

title: fix: dummy reviewer prompt created but output report never materializes
description:
RULES.md:186-219 describes spawning a dummy reviewer that writes output to ${SESSION_DIR}/review-reports/dummy-review-${TIMESTAMP}.md. Both _session-068ecc83 and _session-7edaafbb contain the dummy prompt file (prompts/review-dummy.md) but no corresponding output in review-reports/.

The dummy reviewer output is documented as "discarded" and excluded from Big Head consolidation, so its absence does not affect workflow correctness. However, the context-usage instrumentation goal (measuring baseline token consumption) is silently not achieved.

Fix: Add a note to RULES.md Step 3b-v clarifying expected outcome. If consistently failing to produce output, advance the sunset timeline noted at RULES.md:186.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md documents expected dummy reviewer behavior (output may not appear)
- [ ] If dummy reviewer is sunset, remove the step entirely

Audit reference: DRIFT-030 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: fix: CONTRIBUTING.md cross-file update checklist omits GLOSSARY.md
description:
CONTRIBUTING.md:37-41 lists files to update when adding an agent:
1. README.md "Custom agents" table
2. RULES.md "Agent Types" and "Model Assignments" tables
3. scout.md (noted as usually unnecessary)

Missing: GLOSSARY.md contains an "Ant Metaphor Roles" table (lines 77-85) listing all agents with file paths, model assignments, and role descriptions. Adding an agent without updating this table leaves the GLOSSARY inconsistent.

Fix: Add GLOSSARY.md to the cross-file update checklist.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md checklist includes GLOSSARY.md Ant Metaphor Roles table

Audit reference: DRIFT-029 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Angle-bracket placeholder convention not documented in PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md
Scout briefing format lacks optional Errors section placement
fix: CCO artifact naming uses session-wide format in practice but checkpoints.md specifies per-task
description:
checkpoints.md:179 specifies per-task CCO artifact naming: `pc-{TASK_SUFFIX}-cco-{timestamp}.md` with example at line 28: `pc-74g1-cco-20260215-001145.md`. In practice, both observed sessions use session-wide naming: `pc-session-cco-{timestamp}.md` because the Queen batches all wave prompts into one CCO run.

Root cause: The CCO specification was written assuming one CCO run per task. In practice, the Queen batches all prompts from a wave into a single CCO audit, producing one session-scoped artifact rather than per-task artifacts. The RULES.md Hard Gates table (line 258) uses a wildcard `*-cco-*.md` that accommodates both patterns — the gate works regardless, but the naming spec is misleading.

Current behavior: A future Pest Control agent following checkpoints.md literally would write per-task files. Anyone querying pc/ for `*{TASK_SUFFIX}-cco-*` will find nothing. The wildcard gate still matches, so no runtime failure, but the naming convention is documented incorrectly.

Fix: Update checkpoints.md CCO section to document that batched CCO runs use `pc-session-cco-{timestamp}.md`. Keep per-task naming as valid for single-task CCO runs. Update the example on line 28.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:179: Add batch naming variant
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:28: Update example to show both patterns

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] checkpoints.md documents both per-task and session-wide CCO naming patterns
- [ ] Example on line 28 reflects actual practice
- [ ] Naming convention matches actual artifacts in recent sessions

Audit reference: DRIFT-009 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: README Hard Gates table stale for WWD
description: Root cause: RULES.md Hard Gates table was updated with serial/batch blocking semantics for WWD but README.md:L267-273 still reads 'Next agent in wave' (pre-fix text). Fix: Update README.md Hard Gates WWD row to match RULES.md. Found by: Correctness (CO-F002).

title: Pantry & Scout Template Polish
description: 20 P3 bugs in pantry.md and scout.md agent templates. Covers guard edge cases, fail-fast logic, metadata handling, briefing format, TOCTOU races, and validation gaps.

title: Shell Script Fixes
description: 8 P3 bugs in shell scripts: fill-review-slots.sh, compose-review-skeletons.sh, install-hooks.sh, parse-progress-log.sh, scrub-pii.sh. Comments, error handling, bracket style consistency.

title: [HALF-BAKED] Group co-located tasks into single Dirt Pusher assignments
description:
## Idea

When multiple tasks touch the same file(s), the Scout should group them and assign the group to a single Dirt Pusher instead of spawning separate agents. This avoids git rebase conflicts, gives the agent full context of related changes, and reduces cost/agent count.

The Scout already has the File Modification Matrix (Step 4) that identifies which tasks touch which files — the grouping logic itself is straightforward.

## Motivation

Currently the framework assumes 1 agent = 1 task. When two tasks both modify the same file, they're either serialized into different waves (slow) or run concurrently and risk rebase conflicts. Grouping them into one agent assignment eliminates both problems.

## Blast Radius

This is NOT a small change. The 1-agent-1-task assumption is baked into 6+ components:

| Component | Current assumption | What breaks |
|---|---|---|
| Scout (Step 5) | Wave groupings list task IDs | Needs a "task group" concept — groups of co-located tasks assigned to one agent |
| Pantry | One verdict table row per task, one task brief per agent | Needs to compose combined briefs for task groups |
| Dirt Pusher skeleton | Single {TASK_ID}, single bd close, single summary doc | Needs to iterate: claim all, implement all, close all, write one summary covering multiple tasks |
| Pest Control — DMVDC | Verifies one task's acceptance criteria per agent | Needs to verify multiple tasks' criteria against one agent's commits |
| Pest Control — WWD | Scope check against one task's file list | Scope check against union of grouped tasks' file lists |
| RULES.md | Agent count = task count per wave | Agent count < task count; needs group-aware counting |
| Coverage verification | Counts tasks assigned to waves | Counts tasks assigned to groups assigned to waves |
| Reviews / Big Head | Commit-based, task-agnostic | Minimal impact — reviews look at diffs, not task assignments |

## Key Design Decisions (unresolved)

- Should grouping be mandatory or advisory (Scout recommends, Queen decides)?
- Max tasks per group? (unbounded grouping could overload a single agent)
- How does the summary doc work — one per group or one per task?
- What if grouped tasks have different priorities? (P1 + P3 in same group)
- Does the single-task path need to remain as a separate code path, or can a group of 1 be the universal case?

fix: fill-review-slots.sh @file argument notation undocumented in RULES.md
description:
fill-review-slots.sh (lines 78-94) implements an @file prefix notation allowing multiline arguments to be passed via file paths instead of inline strings. This is particularly useful for <changed-files> and <task-IDs> arguments which often contain newlines.

RULES.md Step 3b-ii (lines 168-170) documents the script invocation with 6 positional arguments but does not mention the @file feature. The core argument count and order match (MATCH on spec), but the convenience feature is undiscoverable from RULES.md alone.

Fix: Optionally add a parenthetical to RULES.md Step 3b-ii noting the @file notation.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md mentions @file prefix for multiline arguments (optional)

Audit reference: DRIFT-031 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: parse-progress-log.sh UNREACHABLE comment reasoning incomplete
fix: MEMORY.md custom agent minimum file requirements TBD caveat may be stale
description:
MEMORY.md:17 states: "Minimum file requirements still TBD — short files (9 lines) failed while full-body agents (200+ lines) work. Needs more testing to find the threshold."

All current agent files exceed 200 lines. CONTRIBUTING.md:33-35 documents the restart requirement but does not mention file size constraints. If the threshold was determined and file size is no longer an issue, the MEMORY.md caveat is misleading. If still unknown, CONTRIBUTING.md is missing a useful warning.

Fix: Determine current status. If file size is no longer a constraint, remove the TBD caveat from MEMORY.md. If still relevant, add a warning to CONTRIBUTING.md.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] MEMORY.md TBD caveat resolved (removed or updated with findings)

Audit reference: DRIFT-028 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: dependency-analysis.md says parallel bd show but scout.md uses sequential write-each-immediately
description: Root cause: orchestration/reference/dependency-analysis.md:58 says '(parallel bd show calls)' but scout.md Step 3 uses sequential write-each-immediately pattern for partial progress safety. Inconsistent guidance. Fix: Qualify or remove 'parallel' mention in dependency-analysis.md to match scout.md's sequential approach. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P3).

title: Installation guide verification test leaves docs/test.md committed permanently
description: Root cause: docs/installation-guide.md:65-70 instructs users to create docs/test.md, commit, and push to verify hooks. This leaves a test file permanently in the repository. Fix: Use a non-committed change or add a cleanup step (git revert). Found by: excellence-reviewer (P3).

title: pantry.md fail-fast conditions use inconsistent signal words
compose-review-skeletons.sh exit 1 in || block redundant with set -euo pipefail
reviews.md section ordering -- Round-Aware Protocol placed after sections that reference it
fix: docs don't clarify code-reviewer is a custom agent outside the repo
description:
checkpoints.md:17 references `code-reviewer` as the agent type Pest Control spawns. It is not a built-in Claude Code agent type — it exists only at ~/.claude/agents/code-reviewer.md (user global), not in the repo's agents/ directory. sync-to-claude.sh does not deploy it, so new adopters on fresh machines would not have it.

Neither checkpoints.md nor SETUP.md clarify this. The Agent Types table in RULES.md:278-286 does not list code-reviewer.

Note: The spawning architecture itself is contradicted by DRIFT-002 (ant-farm-h94m). If Option A is chosen there (remove spawn language), this finding becomes moot. If Option B is chosen (enable spawning), code-reviewer.md should be moved into the repo.

Fix: If the code-reviewer reference is retained, add a note clarifying it is a custom agent requiring manual setup, or move it into the repo agents/ directory.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] code-reviewer deployment path is documented OR agent moved into repo agents/
- [ ] Decision aligns with DRIFT-002 resolution

Audit reference: DRIFT-020 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: big-head-skeleton.md cross-references reviews.md line 322 but model is at line 323
description: Root cause: orchestration/templates/big-head-skeleton.md:18 says 'specified in orchestration/templates/reviews.md line 322; currently opus' but the Model declaration is at line 323 (line 322 is blank). Fix: Change to line 323 or use section reference instead of line number. Found by: correctness-reviewer (P3), excellence-reviewer (P3).

title: Sentinel-file completion protocol for background subagents
description:
When the queen spawns background subagents (run_in_background: true), the only way to retrieve results is TaskOutput, which returns the raw JSONL session transcript — dumping thousands of tokens of hook progress events, tool results, and assistant turns into the queen's context window. This was observed in session 795d29 where Pest Control DMVDC and a dirt pusher were spawned in parallel as background tasks, and TaskOutput injected massive JSONL into the queen's context.

Root cause: TaskOutput returns the full session transcript for background Task agents, not just the agent's final message. Foreground Task calls don't have this problem (they return only the final message), but foreground-only prevents the queen from overlapping work (e.g., preparing next-wave prompts while dirt pushers run).

Interim mitigation: CLAUDE.md currently has a blanket prohibition on run_in_background for the queen. This prevents context leakage but sacrifices the ability to overlap work (e.g., preparing next-wave prompts while dirt pushers run). This bead replaces that blanket ban with a targeted sentinel-file protocol.

Fix: Implement a sentinel-file completion protocol:

1. Session dir setup adds a signals/ directory: mkdir -p ${SESSION_DIR}/{task-metadata,previews,prompts,signals}
2. Agent skeleton template adds a final step: "As your absolute last action, write your verdict summary to {SESSION_DIR}/signals/{TASK_SUFFIX}.done"
3. The .done file contains the agent's compact verdict (PASS/FAIL, file paths, commit hash) — the queen's clean result without JSONL
4. Queen polls for completion via Bash (stat on output file for activity, ls for .done file) — no TaskOutput, no Read on output_file
5. Inactive session + no sentinel = agent crashed, respawn

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md: Add signals/ to session dir setup, add polling protocol, update anti-patterns
- orchestration/templates/dirt-pusher-skeleton.md: Add sentinel write as final step
- CLAUDE.md: Replace blanket run_in_background prohibition with "NEVER use TaskOutput or Read on background agent output files — poll for sentinel files instead"
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md: Pest Control agents also need sentinel writes

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Session dir setup includes signals/ subdirectory
- [ ] dirt-pusher-skeleton.md includes sentinel file write as final step with verdict format
- [ ] CLAUDE.md blanket run_in_background ban replaced with targeted prohibition on TaskOutput/Read of output files, sentinel polling documented
- [ ] RULES.md documents the polling protocol (stat for activity, ls for .done, crash detection)
- [ ] Pest Control and other support agent skeletons include sentinel file write
- [ ] Queen never calls TaskOutput — verified by grep across orchestration templates

title: fix: CLAUDE.md Landing the Plane annotation says Step 6 but content spans Steps 4-6 with gaps
description:
CLAUDE.md:54 annotates the entire 8-step Landing the Plane section with "(Corresponds to RULES.md Step 6.)" — but the content actually spans RULES.md Steps 4, 5, and 6, with material missing in both directions.

RULES.md has steps that CLAUDE.md omits:
- Step 4: Documentation commit (CHANGELOG, README, CLAUDE.md in single commit)
- Step 5: Cross-reference verification

CLAUDE.md has steps that RULES.md omits:
- File issues for remaining work
- Run quality gates
- Review-findings gate (present P1 findings to user)
- Update issue status
- Verify (all changes committed AND pushed)
- Hand off (provide context for next session)

Root cause: The two files evolved independently. CLAUDE.md was extended with operational steps (quality gates, issue management) that RULES.md does not cover, while RULES.md has documentation and verification steps that CLAUDE.md does not include.

Current behavior: A Queen following CLAUDE.md's landing checklist would skip the CHANGELOG documentation commit (RULES.md Step 4) and cross-reference verification (RULES.md Step 5). The false annotation creates confusion about which file is authoritative.

Fix:
1. Update CLAUDE.md:54 annotation to "(Corresponds to RULES.md Steps 4-6.)"
2. Add missing RULES.md steps to CLAUDE.md checklist: documentation commit, cross-reference verification
3. Add missing CLAUDE.md steps to RULES.md: quality gates, issue status updates, git status verification
4. Ensure both files produce the same outcome when followed independently

Changes needed:
- CLAUDE.md:54: Fix annotation
- CLAUDE.md Landing section: Add documentation commit and cross-reference verification steps
- orchestration/RULES.md Steps 4-6: Add quality gates, issue management, git status verification

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] CLAUDE.md annotation correctly references Steps 4-6
- [ ] Both files cover the same complete set of landing steps
- [ ] No step present in one file is absent from the other
- [ ] git status verification appears in both files

Audit reference: DRIFT-007 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: queen-state.md fix commit range has no crash-recovery reconstruction guidance
Fragile grep-based Future Work epic discovery with no error handling or dedup guard
fix: document artifact naming convention transition point for historical sessions
description:
checkpoints.md:27-33 documents the current artifact naming convention: task-specific `pc-{TASK_SUFFIX}-{checkpoint}-{timestamp}.md` and session-wide `pc-session-{checkpoint}-{timestamp}.md` with timestamp format YYYYMMDD-HHmmss (UTC).

Older sessions use different conventions:
- _session-cd9866: Wave-based naming with no timestamps (`pc-session-cco-impl-wave1.md`, `pc-session-wwd-wave2.md`)
- _session-7edaafbb: Mixed — session-scoped DMVDC (`pc-session-dmvdc-*.md`), wave-scoped (`pc-wave2-dmvdc-*.md`), and non-standard compound names (`pc-session-dmvdc-review-fixes-*.md`)
- _session-068ecc83 (most recent): Follows the documented convention correctly

Root cause: The naming convention evolved over sessions. checkpoints.md documents the current standard but does not acknowledge that historical sessions used different formats.

Current behavior: Anyone auditing or recovering historical sessions will find artifacts that do not match the documented naming convention. This is not a bug — it is expected historical variation — but the lack of documentation causes confusion.

Fix: Add a note to checkpoints.md documenting the transition point and historical naming patterns.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md: Add note near naming convention section that sessions prior to _session-068ecc83 used wave-based naming

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] checkpoints.md acknowledges historical naming variation
- [ ] Transition point (_session-068ecc83 as first fully-compliant session) is documented
- [ ] Note clarifies that historical artifacts are expected to diverge from current convention

Audit reference: DRIFT-019 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Pantry empty file list guard does not validate that listed files exist on disk
description: The Pantry empty file list guard (pantry.md:217-228) checks if the Queen-provided file list is empty or whitespace-only, but does not validate that the listed files actually exist on disk. If git state changes between file list generation and Pantry execution (e.g., git reset, branch switch), the Pantry would compose review briefs referencing nonexistent files. This is extremely low likelihood since the Pantry runs immediately after list generation. Affected surfaces: pantry.md:217-228. Fix: Low priority. Optionally add a note: 'The Pantry trusts the Queen file list as authoritative. File existence is verified by Nitpickers at review time.'

Batch mode boundary conditions underdocumented
description: Root cause: RULES.md:L119-131 batch mode documentation does not handle boundary values. (a) Partial wave commit: if some agents crash without committing, no instruction on when to run WWD for committed subset. (b) N=1: single-agent wave technically triggers batch mode but serial mode is simpler. Fix: Add partial commit handling and N=1 clarification to batch mode description. Found by: Edge Cases (EC-02, EC-03), Excellence (EX-F003).

description: RULES.md:407-413 P2 severity examples reference web-frontend concepts (visual regression, accessibility issue) that do not apply to an orchestration-only codebase. Fix: replace with orchestration-relevant examples. Consolidated from: Clarity F4.

title: Commit range and file list validation gaps in RULES.md Step 3b and Pantry guard
scout.md Step 3 does not explicitly say to continue iteration after bd show failure
description: Root cause: orchestration/templates/scout.md:77-80 Step 3 writes metadata per task. Error handling (lines 200-204) says to write error metadata on bd show failure, but Step 3 prose does not say to continue to the next task. A fresh Scout might stop on first failure. Fix: Add 'If bd show fails, write error metadata and continue to next task' in Step 3. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P3).

title: Read Confirmation finding counts can be copied rather than independently verified
Failure taxonomy (INFRASTRUCTURE vs SUBSTANCE) not defined in a shared location
description: The orchestration templates use two failure labels -- INFRASTRUCTURE FAILURE and SUBSTANCE FAILURE -- to distinguish failure root causes. These labels appear across multiple files (pantry.md, big-head-skeleton.md, checkpoints.md, reviews.md) but are never formally defined in one canonical location. A reader encountering one label in isolation has no reference to understand the full taxonomy. Affected surfaces: pantry.md:33/45, big-head-skeleton.md:58, checkpoints.md:333, reviews.md. Fix: Add a brief taxonomy definition in a shared location (e.g., checkpoints.md or a new reference doc) and reference it from each template.

title: Documentation polish: terminology, wording, and examples across review files
description: 9 standalone P3 documentation items grouped for efficiency: (1) parse-progress-log.sh:61-62 step_key terminology inconsistency. (2) RULES.md:158-180 Step numbering collision. (3) checkpoints.md:431 floor-of-3 wording. (4) pantry.md:141-167 missing deprecation note on Step 3.5. (5) reviews.md:11 run-on sentence. (6) checkpoints.md:674-686 missing SSV PASS example. (7) reviews.md:10 timestamp ordering unspecified. (8) checkpoints.md:362-366 DMVDC fallback missing guard. (9) checkpoints.md:670-683 example uses ant-farm- prefix IDs. Fix: Address each in a documentation polish pass. Found by: Clarity (CL-3,CL-4,CL-5,CL-6,CL-7), Correctness (CR-5,CR-6), Edge Cases (EC-5), Excellence (EX-7).

title: Scout metadata write-each-immediately has no truncation/atomicity check downstream
fix: double-brace placeholder tier {{SLOT}} absent from PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md
description:
orchestration/templates/reviews.md:502 uses `{{REVIEW_ROUND}}` — a double-brace placeholder substituted by fill-review-slots.sh before delivery to agents. Line 592 explains: "fill-review-slots.sh substitutes {{REVIEW_ROUND}} with the actual round integer before delivering this brief to Big Head."

PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md defines three tiers:
- Tier 1: {UPPERCASE} — Queen-provided at spawn time
- Tier 2: {lowercase-kebab} — agent-derived at runtime
- Tier 3: ${SHELL_VAR} — bash interpreter

The double-brace {{SLOT}} syntax is a fourth tier substituted by shell scripts (fill-review-slots.sh, compose-review-skeletons.sh) between Queen composition and agent delivery. It is not documented in PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md. Additionally, the File-by-File Audit table in that file marks reviews.md as PASS with "None" for all tiers — factually incorrect since reviews.md uses {{REVIEW_ROUND}}.

Root cause: The double-brace convention was introduced by the review slot-filling scripts without updating the placeholder conventions document.

Fix: Add Tier 4 to PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md and correct the audit table.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/templates/PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md: Add Tier 4 (Script-Substituted {{DOUBLE_BRACE}}) with explanation
- orchestration/templates/PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md: Fix File-by-File Audit table row for reviews.md

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md documents the {{DOUBLE_BRACE}} tier
- [ ] Tier 4 description identifies fill-review-slots.sh as the substitution mechanism
- [ ] File-by-File Audit table for reviews.md reflects the double-brace usage
- [ ] All {{SLOT}} markers across templates are accounted for in the new tier description

title: big-head.md references nonexistent P4 severity level
description:
Root cause: agents/big-head.md:14 uses 'P2 vs P4' as an example of a 2-level severity gap. The project severity scale only defines P1, P2, P3. P4 does not exist. This is internally inconsistent and could confuse Big Head or human readers.

Affected surfaces:
- agents/big-head.md:14

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F11), Excellence (P3 - F5)
Both identified the same P4 reference on the same line.

Fix: Change the example to '(e.g., P1 vs P3)' and remove the P4 reference.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] No references to P4 in big-head.md
- [ ] Example uses only P1-P3 severity levels

title: fix: review-skeletons/ and review-reports/ missing from Step 0 session directory setup
description:
The Step 0 session directory setup in orchestration/RULES.md:336 creates 5 subdirectories:
`mkdir -p "${SESSION_DIR}"/{task-metadata,previews,prompts,pc,summaries}`

Two additional directories appear in every session that reaches the review phase but are not listed:
- review-skeletons/ — created by compose-review-skeletons.sh (called by Pantry in Step 2)
- review-reports/ — created by mkdir -p at RULES.md:175 (Step 3b-iii)

Root cause: These directories were introduced after the original Step 0 setup was written. They are created lazily by their respective workflow phases, but the Session Directory section gives no hint they will exist.

Current behavior: The Step 0 mkdir command is treated as the complete session directory setup. A reader has an incomplete mental model. Crash recovery between Step 2 and Step 3b could fail if it expects these directories to exist before the lazy-creation step runs.

Fix: Add a note after RULES.md:336 documenting the additional directories created during later phases. Do NOT add them to the mkdir command (lazy creation is intentional — sessions that never reach reviews should not have empty review dirs).

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md: Add note after line 336 listing review-skeletons/ and review-reports/ with their creation points

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md Session Directory section documents all 7 subdirectories that appear in practice
- [ ] Note clarifies lazy creation (review dirs created by their respective phases, not at Step 0)
- [ ] Crash recovery documentation accounts for directories that may not yet exist

title: reviews.md polling loop angle-bracket placeholders lack explanatory comment
fix: DMVDC Nitpicker artifact naming in checkpoints.md does not match actual filenames
description:
checkpoints.md:475 documents DMVDC Nitpicker artifact naming as `pc-{TASK_SUFFIX}-dmvdc-review-{timestamp}.md` with example TASK_SUFFIX values like `review-clarity`, `review-edge` (line 478).

Actual filenames in _session-068ecc83/pc/:
- pc-review-correctness-dmvdc-20260221-182700.md
- pc-review-edge-cases-dmvdc-20260221-182700.md

Two deviations from the documented format:
1. The `-review` suffix after `dmvdc` is absent in actual files (documented: `...-dmvdc-review-{ts}`, actual: `...-dmvdc-{ts}`)
2. The TASK_SUFFIX values used are bare review type names (`correctness`, `edge-cases`) rather than the documented compound `review-{type}` (`review-clarity`, `review-edge`)

Root cause: The naming convention in checkpoints.md was written speculatively and never validated against actual Pest Control output.

Current behavior: Anyone querying pc/ for the documented pattern `*dmvdc-review*` will find zero matches. The actual pattern is `*-dmvdc-{timestamp}*` with the review type before `dmvdc`.

Fix: Update checkpoints.md:475 to match actual convention and update example values on line 478.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:475: Change to `pc-{review-type}-dmvdc-{timestamp}.md`
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:478: Update example values to `correctness`, `edge-cases`, `clarity`, `excellence`

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] checkpoints.md DMVDC Nitpicker naming matches actual artifact filenames
- [ ] Example TASK_SUFFIX values match actual Nitpicker review type names
- [ ] Querying pc/ with the documented pattern finds actual files

(b) Bash variable TIMESTAMP (L147-149) differs from template placeholder REVIEW_TIMESTAMP, adding mental overhead. Also: polling loop placeholder at lines 379-382 lacks explicit substitution instruction (low risk, convention established by brief), and escalation format block at lines 540-547 has minor nesting risk. description: The 'On any failure above' block in pantry.md (lines 68-74) with the partial verdict table appears after Condition 3 at the same indentation level. It is ambiguous whether this block runs once after processing ALL tasks (intended behavior) or after EACH individual failure (mid-loop). The placement after Condition 3 could be read as specific to that condition rather than a general end-of-loop instruction. Affected surfaces: pantry.md:68-74. Fix: Move the partial verdict table instruction to after the per-task loop ends and clarify: 'After processing all tasks, if any failures occurred, return a single partial verdict table.'

title: dirt-pusher-skeleton.md has policy text embedded in placeholder list
description: The AGENT_TYPE placeholder entry at dirt-pusher-skeleton.md:19-22 includes a multi-line authority chain and override policy, making the compact list harder to scan. Fix: extract policy into separate subsection. Consolidated from: Clarity F9.

Review Pipeline Polish
description: 35 P3 bugs across reviews.md, big-head.md, big-head-skeleton.md, nitpicker.md, dirt-pusher-skeleton.md, CCB/DMVDC verification. Covers review rounds, gates, failure paths, step numbering, severity levels, and cross-file consistency.

nitpicker.md cross-review messaging section placed after per-type blocks

Root cause: nitpicker.md:162-170 'Cross-Review Messaging' section is placed after the 4 type-specific specialization blocks. Instructions at line 37 say 'Read the block that matches your assigned review type. Ignore the other three blocks entirely.' A strict reader would stop before reaching line 162. The cross-review messaging is universally relevant.

Affected surfaces:
- agents/nitpicker.md:162-170

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F3). Single reviewer, standalone finding.

Fix: Move Cross-Review Messaging section up to follow after Shared Rules (after line 31), before Per-Review-Type Specialization heading.

title: fix: MEMORY.md Project Structure still lists colony-tsa.md as being eliminated

MEMORY.md Project Structure section (line 28) lists:
`orchestration/templates/colony-tsa.md — Colony TSA (being eliminated, see HANDOFF)`

The colony-tsa.md file was archived months ago at orchestration/_archive/colony-tsa.md. The "Completed: Colony TSA Eliminated" section later in the same MEMORY.md correctly records the completion. The Project Structure section was never updated.

Fix: Update the Project Structure section to reflect the archived path and completed status.

fix: remove archived pantry-review from scout.md exclusion list
description:
orchestration/templates/scout.md:63 lists `pantry-review` in the agent exclusion list: "scout-organizer, pantry-impl, pantry-review, pest-control, nitpicker, big-head". The pantry-review agent is archived (orchestration/_archive/pantry-review.md) and has no file in agents/.

GLOSSARY.md correctly uses strikethrough for pantry-review. pantry.md correctly warns against spawning it. Only the scout.md exclusion list still references it as if it were active.

Since pantry-review has no agent file in agents/, it would not appear in the Scout's catalog anyway — the reference is harmless but signals the list was not updated on deprecation.

Fix: Remove `pantry-review` from the exclusion list in scout.md:63.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] scout.md exclusion list no longer references pantry-review

Audit reference: DRIFT-021 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Pantry failure artifact path collision across conditions for same task
description: All three Pantry failure conditions (Condition 1: missing files, Condition 2: incomplete metadata, Condition 3: placeholder contamination) write their failure artifacts to the same path: {session-dir}/prompts/task-{TASK_SUFFIX}-FAILED.md. While only one condition fires per task in the current sequential waterfall, the shared path means: (1) the failure type is only distinguishable by reading the artifact content, not the filename; (2) a future refactoring that makes checks non-sequential could cause silent overwrites. Affected surfaces: pantry.md:33, pantry.md:47, pantry.md:58. Fix: Low priority. Add a comment documenting the sequential-check invariant. Optionally add condition type to filename.

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md Enforcement Strategy mixes completed and aspirational items
pantry-review.md self-validation checklist lacks remediation guidance per item
Standalone documentation polish items (4 sub-issues)
description: Four independent P3 documentation gaps grouped for filing efficiency: (a) SSV acronym not expanded at first use in RULES.md:L88 Step 1b header. (b) Shell quoting error in CONTRIBUTING.md:L147 example (backslash-n in double quotes is literal, not newline). (c) Ambiguous priority labels on Nitpicker specializations in GLOSSARY.md:L84. (d) No-delete rsync consequence undocumented in CONTRIBUTING.md:L161 (stale files accumulate in ~/.claude/). Found by: Clarity (CL-F002, CL-F003, CL-F004), Excellence (EX-F004).

title: PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md not cross-referenced from RULES.md or any template

description:
Root cause: pantry.md:238-243 Section 2 deprecation notice says 'superseded by the two-script approach introduced in Section 1 Step 2.5' without naming the scripts. The term 'two-script approach' appears only once in the file with no definition or link.

Affected surfaces:
- orchestration/templates/pantry.md:238-243

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F7). Single reviewer, standalone finding.

Fix: Add script names parenthetically: 'the two-script approach (compose-review-skeletons.sh + fill-review-slots.sh)'.

Acceptance criteria:

title: scout.md PICK ONE bracket syntax resembles regex/BNF notation
description:
Root cause: scout.md:118-119 uses 'PICK ONE: [type-a | type-b]' format for tie-breaking results. The bracket-pipe syntax resembles regex or BNF notation and could be confused with optional/alternative notation rather than a literal list.

Affected surfaces:
- orchestration/templates/scout.md:118-119

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F6). Single reviewer, standalone finding.

Fix: Use plainer format like 'PICK ONE: debugger OR performance-engineer'.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Tie-breaking format uses plain English connectors instead of bracket notation

title: fix: one-TeamCreate-per-session constraint undocumented in operator-facing docs
description:
Claude Code supports only one TeamCreate call per session. This constraint shapes the entire review architecture: Pest Control is included as a Nitpicker team member (rather than spawned separately after the team completes) because a second TeamCreate for a standalone PC spawn would fail at runtime.

The constraint is documented only in MEMORY.md:19-21: "Claude Code only supports one TeamCreate per session. The Nitpicker team (4 reviewers + Big Head) uses this slot. QA coordination (Pantry ↔ Pest Control) must use file-based handoff, not teams."

Root cause: The constraint was discovered empirically and captured in MEMORY.md but never propagated to RULES.md, CLAUDE.md, or CONTRIBUTING.md.

Current behavior: An operator extending the workflow (e.g., trying to create a second team for QA coordination) will encounter a runtime failure with no prior warning in any authoritative doc. The architectural rationale for why PC is inside the Nitpicker team is invisible to anyone reading RULES.md.

Fix: Document the constraint in RULES.md near the Nitpicker team setup.

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md Step 3b-iv (near team setup): Add note explaining one-TeamCreate-per-session constraint and why PC is a team member

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md documents the one-TeamCreate-per-session constraint
- [ ] Note explains the architectural implication (PC must be team member, not separate spawn)
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md or SETUP.md mentions the constraint for framework extenders

fix: README shows 5-member Nitpicker team but RULES.md requires 6 (Pest Control inside team)
description:
README.md:59 describes the Nitpicker team as "4 reviewers + Big Head" (5 members). README.md:218 shows the flow: "create Nitpicker team (4 reviewers + Big Head)" with the Queen spawning a separate Pest Control after the team completes (line 201).

RULES.md:179 specifies a 6-member team: "Round 1: 6 members — 4 reviewers + Big Head + Pest Control." RULES.md:182 explains why: "Pest Control MUST be a team member so Big Head can SendMessage to it." RULES.md:184 confirms: "After team completes, DMVDC and CCB have already run inside the team."

Root cause: README was written before Pest Control was added as a team member. The architectural change (driven by the one-TeamCreate-per-session constraint — see DRIFT-015) was applied to RULES.md but not propagated to the README.

Current behavior: An operator following the README Step 3b flow would spawn a 5-member team and then separately spawn Pest Control. Big Head would be unable to SendMessage to PC (different team/no team), breaking the team-internal DMVDC/CCB flow.

Fix: Update README architecture diagram, team description, and flow diagram to reflect 6-member team with PC inside.

Changes needed:
- README.md:59: Change to "4 reviewers + Big Head + Pest Control"
- README.md:218: Update flow diagram to show 6-member team
- README.md:201: Remove separate PC spawn; note DMVDC/CCB run inside team

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] README describes 6-member Nitpicker team (4 reviewers + Big Head + Pest Control)
- [ ] Flow diagram shows PC as team member, not separate spawn
- [ ] No reference to spawning PC separately after team completes

Audit reference: DRIFT-016 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: fix: SSV checkpoint missing from RULES.md Model Assignments table
description:
The Model Assignments table in orchestration/RULES.md:288-304 lists model assignments for all Pest Control checkpoint types (CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB) but has no row for SSV (Scout Strategy Verification). The table header at RULES.md:290 states: "Every Task tool call the Queen makes MUST include the model parameter from this table. Omitting model causes the agent to inherit the Queen's opus model, wasting tokens on agents that don't need it."

Root cause: When SSV was added as a checkpoint, it was documented inline in Step 1b (RULES.md:89 — `spawn Pest Control (pest-control, model: "haiku") for Scout Strategy Verification`) and in checkpoints.md:612 (`Model: haiku`), but the Model Assignments table was not updated.

Current behavior: A Queen following the table as the authoritative model reference will find no SSV entry and must hunt in Step 1b prose. Three independent audit agents flagged this same gap.

Fix: Add a row to the Model Assignments table between the current first and second rows:
`| PC — SSV | Task (pest-control) | haiku | Pure set comparisons — no judgment required |`

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md:297 (insert new row in Model Assignments table)

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Model Assignments table includes PC — SSV row with model haiku
- [ ] All 5 PC checkpoint types (SSV, CCO, WWD, DMVDC, CCB) have table entries
- [ ] Table row note matches checkpoints.md:612 rationale

Audit reference: DRIFT-005 from docs/audits/2026-02-21-orchestration-drift-audit.md

title: Pantry fail-fast says Halt but behavior is skip-to-next-task
description: Line 30 of pantry.md says 'FAIL-FAST CHECK: Halt and report for any of these conditions.' The word 'Halt' implies stopping all processing. But the actual behavior in Conditions 1-3 is per-task skipping: 'Do not proceed with task brief composition for this task' and 'skip to the next task.' The Pantry continues processing remaining tasks after a failure. The 'Halt' wording became more visibly misleading after ant-farm-zeu added detailed per-condition behavior (write artifact, skip to next). Affected surfaces: pantry.md:30-31. Fix: Change 'Halt and report' to 'Check and skip on failure' or 'Validate before proceeding.'

title: Explore tmux-based agent spawning for real-time observability
description: Explore running orchestrated subagents in tmux windows instead of background Task agents, so you can observe agent work in real-time. Key research questions: (1) Can the boss-bot launch claude CLI sessions in tmux windows via Bash? (2) How to pipe prompts into those sessions? (3) How to collect results back? (4) Hook-based automation options? Goal: Each spawned agent gets its own tmux window you can switch to with ctrl+b n/p. May influence orchestration templates if feasible. (Moved from Documentation Gaps epic ant-farm-9wq)

title: RULES.md round 2+ composition phrasing inconsistency with reviews.md

bd show failure guard only on DMVDC Check 2, missing from other bd show callsites
description: The new bd show failure guard (ant-farm-zeu) is placed only on DMVDC Check 2 (checkpoints.md:332-337). However, other checkpoints also invoke bd show: WWD (line 245) and CCB Check 2 (lines 490-492). These callsites lack equivalent failure guards. If bd show can fail for DMVDC, it can fail for WWD and CCB too. Affected surfaces: checkpoints.md:245 (WWD), checkpoints.md:490-492 (CCB Check 2). Fix: Add similar guards to all bd show callsites, or create a shared 'bd show failure handling' note in the Pest Control Overview section.
